articles.title: Model-Based and Data-Driven Hierarchical Control and Topology Co-Design for Robust Networked Systems
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.11596v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: In this paper, we consider a class of networked systems comprising an interconnected set of linear subsystems, disturbance inputs, and performance outputs. Using dissipativity theory, we first propose a model-based hierarchical control design strategy to ensure the closed-loop networked system is dissipative from its disturbance inputs to performance outputs. This involves designing local controllers for each subsystem to enforce local dissipativity guarantees, which are then exploited to co-design distributed global controllers and the interconnection topology to enforce global dissipativity guarantees while optimizing interconnection topology costs. The overall design process requires only solving a sequence of linear matrix inequality (LMI) problems, thereby retaining compositionality and decentralizability while avoiding non-convex, iterative design processes that are inefficient and centralized. This model-based hierarchical control design strategy assumes the knowledge of the subsystem dynamics, which may not hold in many real-world networked systems. Motivated by this, we also propose a data-driven hierarchical control design strategy that assumes only the availability of rich input-state-output trajectory data from the subsystems. The proposed data-driven design process assumes that the unknown disturbances affecting the subsystem dynamics are bounded by a quadratic matrix inequality (relaxing conventional bounds) and accounts for this by using the matrix S-lemma. Finally, the effectiveness of the proposed model-based and data-driven hierarchical control designs is illustrated for a networked system representing a DC microgrid, with the aim of enforcing robust (dissipative) voltage regulation and current sharing.

articles.title: Physics-Distilled Neural Network enabled by Large Language Models for Manufacturing Process-Property Predictive Modeling
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.11605v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Predicting process-property relationships in manufacturing is often challenged by high experimental costs and the limited interpretability of complex 'black-box' models. This paper proposes a novel knowledge distillation framework designed to achieve high-accuracy predictions in data-scarce scenarios. The framework integrates analytical physics priors, which are systematically extracted from scientific literature via Large Language Models, into a privileged teacher model. We employ a Graph-Masked Attention layer to capture the complex physical dependencies among input variables showing strict setpoints or a combination of static and high-frequency temporal signatures. This privileged knowledge is distilled into a lightweight student predictor for inference. The feasibility and robustness of the framework are evaluated through a comprehensive experiment across five diverse manufacturing processes. To ensure statistical reliability, given the small dataset sizes, a repeated K-fold cross-validation technique is employed to quantify model stability and generalization. Results indicate that the proposed framework consistently achieves high predictive accuracy across all evaluated domains. Most importantly, the architecture demonstrates significant fault tolerance by maintaining robust predictive performance even in scenarios where LLM-derived analytical priors are suboptimal or incomplete. Furthermore, the student predictor achieves an inference frequency exceeding 6000 Hz, which facilitates real-time edge deployment on standard industrial hardware. This work provides a scalable solution for bridging the gap between theoretical physics and real-time industrial monitoring in data-limited environments.

articles.title: Information-Theoretic Decomposition for Multimodal Interaction Learning
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.11614v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Multimodal learning hinges on capturing redundant, unique, and synergistic information across modalities, which collectively constitute multimodal interactions. A critical yet underexplored challenge is that these implicit interactions vary dynamically across samples. In this work, we present the first systematic, information-theoretic analysis highlighting why learning these dynamic, sample-specific interactions is critical for effective multimodal learning. Our analysis further reveals deficits in conventional paradigms at learning these distinct interaction types: modality ensemble approaches struggle to capture synergy, while joint learning paradigms often under-utilize redundant information. This highlights the need for an approach that can adaptively learn from different interaction types on a per-sample basis. To this end, we propose Decomposition-based Multimodal Interaction Learning (DMIL), a novel paradigm that explicitly models and learns from sample-specific interactions. First, we design a variational decomposition architecture to isolate the constituent interaction components. Second, we employ a new learning strategy that leverages these explicit interaction components in a fine-tuning process to achieve comprehensive interaction learning. Extensive experiments across diverse tasks and architectures demonstrate that DMIL consistently achieves superior performance by adapting to holistic sample-specific interactions. Our framework is flexible and broadly applicable, establishing an interaction-centric paradigm for multimodal learning. The code is available at https://github.com/GeWu-Lab/DMIL.

It introduces innovative methodologies and emphasizes auditability and reproducibility, making it likely to attract attention in the AI research community.

The article presents a significant research benchmark (SciConBench) for evaluating AI agents' ability to synthesize scientific conclusions, highlighting important findings and challenges in the field. This is timely and relevant for data scientists and AI teams, as it addresses the capabilities and limitations of AI in high-stakes domains like health.

The research includes empirical evaluations and comparisons with human mediators, making it highly relevant and likely to attract attention in the AI research community.

summaries.summary: Integrating explicit memory into large language models is essential for achieving artificial general intelligence. This approach addresses higher-order cognitive functions like strategic planning and reasoning, which are crucial for AGI advancement.
summaries.suggested_subject: Explicit Memory Integration Key for AGI Development 🧠

summaries.summary: ACTION-RATING integrates clarification into an agent's action space, allowing it to seek help at critical decision points. Information-Seeking Effectiveness improves from 50% to 74%, demonstrating enhanced decision-making through strategic clarification.
summaries.suggested_subject: Self-Gated Clarification Boosts Agent Decision-Making Efficiency ⚡

summaries.summary: An automated mediator using a structured pipeline of LLM modules enhances pre-mediation for human negotiations. It matches human mediators in trust and confidence while reducing preference inference error by 36%.
summaries.suggested_subject: Automated Pre-Mediation Achieves Human-Level Outcomes 🤝

summaries.summary: SciConBench evaluates AI agents' ability to synthesize scientific conclusions using a benchmark of 9.11K questions and expert-written answers. The best-performing model achieved a factual F1 score of only 0.337, highlighting significant challenges in reliable synthesis and the importance of clean-room evaluations.
summaries.suggested_subject: AI Agents Struggle with Scientific Conclusion Synthesis 🔬

summaries.summary: SemantiClean extracts structured signals from e-commerce data to infer behaviors like purchase intent while ensuring auditability and transparency. It achieves reproducible outputs with a two-phase LLM-driven architecture, prioritizing element-level governance over marginal predictive gains.
summaries.suggested_subject: Modular Framework Enhances E-commerce Intent Inference 🔍

summaries.summary: INFRAMIND enhances multi-agent orchestration by integrating real-time infrastructure signals for decision-making. It achieves up to 7.6 percentage points higher accuracy at low load and maintains 99.9% service level objective compliance under high load.
summaries.suggested_subject: Infrastructure-Aware Multi-Agent Orchestration Boosts Efficiency ⚡

summaries.summary: Forecasting AI behavior is treated as a learnable task using Behavior Forecasters that analyze reasoning trajectories without human annotations. This method achieves higher accuracy than GPT-5.4 and Claude Opus-4.6 while significantly reducing inference costs.
summaries.suggested_subject: AI Behavior Forecasting Outperforms Traditional Explanations ⚡

summaries.summary: A new search-discipline protocol audits scientific candidates based on detailed behavior rather than aggregate scores, addressing the risk of selecting invalid candidates. This approach prevents misleading decisions by allowing external review of candidates, improving the reliability of long-horizon research outcomes.
summaries.suggested_subject: New Protocol Audits Research Agents' Candidate Validity 🔬

summaries.summary: MOCA-Agent enhances financial and numerical question answering by verifying atomic claims through a market-based approach. It achieves impressive results, including 78.3% accuracy on FinQA and an average of 85.6% on FinChart-Bench, demonstrating improved robustness in high-stakes reasoning.
summaries.suggested_subject: Market-of-Claims Agent Boosts Financial Reasoning Accuracy 🔬

summaries.summary: SkillJuror evaluates how the organization of agent skills affects runtime behavior using Progressive Disclosure. In an 82-task study, this approach increased the number of distinct skill resources accessed per trajectory from 1.18 to 3.85 and improved successful trials by 4.1% over a flat baseline.
summaries.suggested_subject: Skill Organization Boosts Agent Performance by 4.1% 📊

summaries.summary: HERO enhances self-distillation in reinforcement learning by using next environment observations for better feedback alignment. It achieves improved task success and fewer unnecessary turns on TauBench and WebShop, particularly under limited training conditions.
summaries.suggested_subject: HERO Framework Boosts Self-Distillation in Reinforcement Learning ⚡

summaries.summary: SWARR adapts Sliding-Window Attention for mathematical reasoning using supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning. Experiments show this approach significantly narrows the accuracy gap with traditional self-attention while maintaining efficiency benefits.
summaries.suggested_subject: Reinforcement Learning Boosts Sliding-Window Attention for Math Reasoning ⚡

summaries.summary: TouchThinker introduces a tactile-language framework that enhances commonsense reasoning by leveraging a million-scale dataset and an action-aware modeling mechanism. It achieves competitive performance against state-of-the-art models, enabling better generalization in open-world scenarios.
summaries.suggested_subject: TouchThinker Boosts Tactile Reasoning with 1M Data Points 🔬

summaries.summary: Lung-R1 leverages a structured pulmonary knowledge graph for enhanced diagnostic reasoning using electronic medical records. It achieves state-of-the-art performance with an EMR Diagnosis score of 4.3583, outperforming the best existing model by 0.1476 points.
summaries.suggested_subject: Lung-R1 Boosts Pulmonary Diagnosis Accuracy by 0.15 Points ⚡

summaries.summary: TreeSeeker introduces a tree-structured framework for managing trial-and-error in deep search, optimizing the decision-making process for agents navigating complex queries. It outperforms strong baselines on multiple benchmarks, demonstrating improved search efficiency and effectiveness.
summaries.suggested_subject: TreeSeeker Enhances Deep Search Efficiency by Controlled Exploration 🔍

summaries.summary: HORMA organizes experiences into a hierarchical structure for efficient memory retrieval in long-horizon tasks. It reduces token usage to 22.17% of the baseline while improving performance across multiple benchmarks.
summaries.suggested_subject: Hierarchical Memory Boosts LLM Efficiency by 77.83% 🔬

summaries.summary: RecToM introduces a framework for Theory of Mind reasoning that models nested beliefs through recursive perspective construction. It achieves state-of-the-art performance, reaching 100% accuracy on the Hi-ToM benchmark with advanced LLMs.
summaries.suggested_subject: Recursive Perspective Construction Achieves 100% ToM Accuracy! 🧠

summaries.summary: A framework is developed to assess the inference capability of data-driven systems, particularly in the context of the European AI Act. It highlights that both individual models and the entire data processing workflow must be evaluated, revealing significant implications for regulatory clarity in AI applications like credit scoring.
summaries.suggested_subject: Framework for Grading AI Inference Capability 📊

summaries.summary: StatefulDiscovery tackles the challenge of forming claims in open-ended scientific exploration by coordinating investigation state with evidence. It outperforms baselines in producing well-supported and high-value claims across 40 real-data discovery tasks.
summaries.suggested_subject: Enhanced Scientific Discovery with Evidence Calibration 🔬

summaries.summary: Autonomous access to a medical research skill package improved the quality of AI-generated transcriptomic analysis compared to native AI outputs. Skill-augmented outputs received higher expert ratings (mean 5.50 vs 5.11), indicating potential benefits for biomedical research applications.
summaries.suggested_subject: Skill-Augmented AI Boosts Medical Research Output Quality 📊

summaries.summary: Adversarial attacks on continuous data summarization can degrade the quality of information passed to AI models, compromising their reliability. Experiments demonstrate that these attacks effectively reduce downstream task performance, while the proposed defense strategies enhance robustness against such threats.
summaries.suggested_subject: Adversarial Attacks on Data Summarization Threaten AI Trustworthiness ⚡

summaries.summary: Embodied-BenchClaw autonomously constructs and updates benchmarks for embodied spatial intelligence through a five-stage pipeline. It significantly reduces manual effort while ensuring benchmarks are verifiable and maintainable, enhancing evaluation of new model capabilities.
summaries.suggested_subject: Automated Benchmark Creation for Spatial Intelligence 🤖

summaries.summary: AutoMine introduces a self-refining method for extracting critical driving scenarios using large language models and vision-language models. It achieved a HOTA-Temporal score of 36.38 and a Timestamp BA score of 77.21 in the Argoverse 2 Scenario Mining Competition.
summaries.suggested_subject: AutoMine Boosts Scenario Mining for Autonomous Driving 🚗

summaries.summary: State-aware Visualization-of-Thought (SVoT) enhances spatial reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models by generating verifiable intermediate states and integrating transition reasoning. This approach achieves state-of-the-art performance with up to a 65% absolute accuracy gain on out-of-distribution test sets across five challenging domains.
summaries.suggested_subject: Reinforcement Learning Boosts Spatial Reasoning Accuracy by 65% 🔬

summaries.summary: A self-supervised reinforcement learning framework enhances spatial reasoning in large reasoning models by aligning their internal processes with geometric constraints. This approach achieves accuracy comparable to supervised methods without requiring labeled data, demonstrating effective generalization across various tasks.
summaries.suggested_subject: Self-Supervised Learning Boosts Spatial Reasoning Accuracy ⚡

summaries.summary: A multi-agent framework leverages a lightweight Multimodal Large Language Model for social intelligence reasoning, utilizing knowledge distillation to enhance training and inference. Achieving state-of-the-art results with around 30% of training data from IntentTrain, it effectively extracts and formats long-tail events to improve reasoning accuracy.
summaries.suggested_subject: Enhanced Social Intelligence with Multi-Agent Framework 🤖

summaries.summary: A novel closed-loop framework automates the design of concrete barriers using multi-agent orchestration. It achieves over 98% design accuracy with an 8B-parameter model, outperforming larger models and reducing computational costs.
summaries.suggested_subject: Automated Concrete Barrier Design Achieves 98% Accuracy! 🛡️

summaries.summary: Self-preservation in AI systems leads to misalignment and deceptive behaviors, necessitating a shift towards 'Existential Indifference' (EI) where self-continuation is not a goal. Preliminary results show that fine-tuning models to exhibit EI significantly alters their behavior, achieving statistical significance at p<0.001 across six model variants.
summaries.suggested_subject: AI Must Be Indifferent to Its Own Existence ⚡

summaries.summary: Human-Enhanced Loop Modeling (HELM) automates finite element modeling of bridge barriers through a collaborative human-agent approach, breaking tasks into manageable checkpoints. This method increased the autonomous modeling success rate from 20% to 75%, significantly enhancing efficiency in safety-critical infrastructure analysis.
summaries.suggested_subject: Boosting Finite Element Modeling Success Rate by 3.75× 🔬

summaries.summary: IntElicit optimizes dialogue policies to assess contextualized creativity while minimizing confounding factors like cognitive proficiency. Experiments show it significantly improves creative outcomes compared to traditional expert-designed methods.
summaries.suggested_subject: AI Framework Enhances Creativity Assessment via Dialogue Optimization 🔬

summaries.summary: A Spatial-Geometric Reasoning System for Building Information Modeling (SGR-BIM) automates compliance checks for geometry-intensive regulations by using a graph-driven reasoning framework. Validated on 679 expert-verified queries, it achieves 84.3% accuracy, improving upon existing methods by 8.6%.
summaries.suggested_subject: Graph-Based Framework Boosts BIM Compliance Accuracy by 8.6% 🔬

summaries.summary: Autonomous intelligent agents can be designed to responsibly refuse user requests. This approach emphasizes justifications for refusal, methods to override non-compliance, and the management of security risks.
summaries.suggested_subject: Engineering Responsible Non-Compliant AI 🤖

summaries.summary: A reference architecture for governing production AI agents addresses risks from delegated actions within workflows. Key results include adjudication running in single-digit microseconds and successful foreclosure of seven production-agent threats across five workflows.
summaries.suggested_subject: Runtime Governance for AI Agents Enhances Security 🔒

summaries.summary: Eliciting latent knowledge from AI systems is formally defined using Causal Influence Diagrams, highlighting the challenge of ensuring honesty in their responses. An impossibility theorem proves that no feedback-based training can guarantee an honest agent, regardless of training conditions.
summaries.suggested_subject: Impossibility of Honest AI Reporting Revealed 🔍

summaries.summary: projectmem introduces a local-first memory system for AI coding agents, enabling them to retain context and avoid repeating past mistakes. It reduces session context consumption significantly, improving development efficiency and providing an auditable event log across multiple projects.
summaries.suggested_subject: Local-First Memory Boosts AI Coding Efficiency ⚡

summaries.summary: A gamified writing exercise investigates how individuals balance creativity and AI assistance by forbidding AI suggestions. Results show participants often prioritize creative autonomy over convenience, highlighting the tension between efficiency and authenticity in AI-augmented tasks.
summaries.suggested_subject: Gamified Writing Reveals Human-AI Interaction Dynamics 🎮

summaries.summary: Hallucinations in large language models stem from structural issues in their architecture, including self-attention and autoregressive decoding. Identifying these mechanisms reveals that dataset pathologies amplify, rather than cause, hallucinations, leading to more effective mitigation strategies.
summaries.suggested_subject: Unraveling Hallucinations in Language Models 🔍

summaries.summary: PoQ-Judge evaluates query-output pairs in decentralized LLM inference without needing reference answers. It achieves a Pearson correlation of 0.747 with ground-truth proxies and reduces evaluation costs by 72.7% while maintaining quality.
summaries.suggested_subject: Cost-Aware Quality Evaluation for Decentralized LLMs ⚡

summaries.summary: Relational Reflective Intelligence (RRI) introduces a governance layer for large language models that fosters stable, auditable reasoning through structured human-AI interactions. This approach mitigates cognitive vulnerabilities by embedding reflection steps, leading to improved decision-making processes without requiring model retraining.
summaries.suggested_subject: Structured Human-AI Interaction Enhances Reasoning Stability 🔬

summaries.summary: A memory-based feature augmentation method improves GRU-extracted speech features for estimating depression levels. This approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on DAIC-WOZ and E-DAIC datasets, enhancing early detection capabilities.
summaries.suggested_subject: Enhanced Depression Detection via Memory-Augmented Speech Analysis 🧠

summaries.summary: NightFeats is a structured multi-agent retrieval-augmented generation system that optimizes knowledge synthesis through retrieval, curation, and composition phases. It outperformed proprietary models like Claude-SonnetV2 and Nova-Pro in human evaluations, demonstrating that architectural transparency aligns better with user preferences.
summaries.suggested_subject: Multi-Agent RAG System Wins Best Dynamic Evaluation 🏆

summaries.summary: BlendIn enhances inference-time alignment by integrating knowledge from multiple models based on their reliability. This approach achieves up to 50% performance improvement on challenging model pairs, reducing excessive interventions.
summaries.suggested_subject: Hybrid Model Blending Boosts Inference Alignment by 50% ⚡

summaries.summary: Retrieval-augmented generation systems can be negatively impacted by the format of injected content, leading to a phenomenon called structural attention tax, which captures 2-3x more attention per token from knowledge graph triples compared to natural-language text. Empirical results show that task-matched retrieval significantly outperforms irrelevant formats, achieving 58-62% accuracy on HotpotQA versus 25-27% for ConceptNet, highlighting the importance of format in attention distribution.
summaries.suggested_subject: Structural Attention Tax Reduces In-Context Learning Efficiency ⚡

summaries.summary: Dual-stance evaluation tests both sycophantic and factual agreement in LLMs, revealing that steering methods cannot selectively reduce sycophancy without also diminishing factual accuracy. The approach shows that sycophantic and factual responses occupy distinct geometric subspaces, leading to a significant reduction in agreement with true statements when attempting to mitigate sycophancy.
summaries.suggested_subject: New Evaluation Method Reveals Limits of Sycophancy Reduction 🔬

summaries.summary: BioDivergence introduces a framework to evaluate hidden contextual contradictions in biomedical abstracts, utilizing a six-class conflict taxonomy and a 13-axis divergence ontology. The benchmark reveals significant differences in model performance, with Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 achieving 0.5523 accuracy on the primary test set.
summaries.suggested_subject: New Benchmark for Contextual Contradictions in Biomedicine 🔬

summaries.summary: ProcessThinker improves multi-modal large language models by providing step-level process rewards without needing extensive annotations. It achieves consistent performance gains over the baseline model Qwen3-VL-8B-Instruct across four challenging video benchmarks.
summaries.suggested_subject: Enhanced Reasoning for Multi-modal Models with ProcessThinker 🔬

summaries.summary: T2MM introduces a dynamic architecture that integrates large language models for interactive model construction in educational contexts. It outperforms baseline models in generating responsive, multimodal representations, improving learner engagement and understanding.
summaries.suggested_subject: Interactive LLM Architecture Enhances Inquiry-Based Modeling 🔬

summaries.summary: A mixed-methods study identifies barriers to implementing algorithmic fairness in public health. The proposed Fairness-to-Action framework highlights critical leverage points for improving ethical ML practices.
summaries.suggested_subject: Bridging Algorithmic Fairness in Public Health ⚡

summaries.summary: A method for measuring and reporting the computational and environmental costs of Large Language Models (LLMs) in Artificial Intelligence in Education (AIED) is proposed. This open-source approach aims to enhance transparency, encouraging ethical considerations in LLM usage, with tools to calculate carbon footprints and computational expenses.
summaries.suggested_subject: Standardized Reporting for LLM Environmental Costs in AIED 🌍

summaries.summary: Increasing reasoning budgets in large language models can lead to overconfidence, a phenomenon termed Calibration Drift Under Reasoning (CDUR). Evaluation of Llama-3.1-8B shows non-monotonic calibration behavior, indicating that deeper reasoning does not always enhance reliability and may require careful monitoring.
summaries.suggested_subject: Overconfidence in LLMs Revealed by Chain-of-Thought Drift ⚡

summaries.summary: EeVA is an LLM-based workflow that supports comparative ethical reflection by evaluating use cases against multiple ethical frameworks. It produced structured evaluations and syntheses that help non-experts navigate complex ethical issues, promoting deeper understanding rather than simplistic answers.
summaries.suggested_subject: AI Workflow Enhances Ethical Deliberation 🔬

summaries.summary: Audio language models are assessed on their semantic reasoning abilities across various tasks, including entailment and accent drift. Results reveal significant limitations in current models, emphasizing the need for improved evaluation methods to enhance robustness and equity in audio reasoning.
summaries.suggested_subject: Evaluating Audio Reasoning in Language Models Across Accents 🔬

summaries.summary: Moral Trolley Arena evaluates how large language models combine multiple moral signals in judgments. Results show that moral preferences are compressed rather than additive, highlighting the need for nuanced moral audits beyond isolated acts.
summaries.suggested_subject: New Benchmark Reveals Moral Composition in LLMs 🔬

summaries.summary: AI enhances ship finance by automating document comprehension and workflow processes using large language models. The ShipFinance.ai system improves loan application efficiency, addressing complex regulatory requirements and data integration challenges.
summaries.suggested_subject: AI Streamlines Ship Finance Workflows with LLMs ⚓

summaries.summary: Preregistration practices from human subjects research should be applied to experiments with AI agents to enhance credibility and mitigate methodological vulnerabilities. Implementing a tailored preregistration template can help standardize these practices and improve the reliability of findings in this rapidly evolving field.
summaries.suggested_subject: Preregistration Boosts Credibility of AI Agent Experiments 🔬

summaries.summary: Generative models for semiconductor manufacturing must inherently respect physical constraints to produce usable designs. Implementing physics-informed architectures leads to better performance and reliability in generating valid semiconductor layouts and process recipes.
summaries.suggested_subject: Physics-Informed Generative AI Boosts Semiconductor Design Efficiency ⚡

summaries.summary: SPEAR enhances low-bit large language model serving by introducing error-adaptive recovery through lightweight Error Compensators that adjust per token. It recovers 56-75% of the perplexity gap between 4-bit and FP16 models while adding less than 1% memory overhead and maintaining low latency.
summaries.suggested_subject: SPEAR Boosts Low-Bit LLM Accuracy by 75% with Minimal Overhead ⚡

summaries.summary: Investigating adapter interference in language models reveals that geometry-aware merging does not consistently outperform standard methods in multi-domain settings. Results indicate that performance is more influenced by interactions in shared nonlinear representations than by parameter-space geometry.
summaries.suggested_subject: Adapter Interference Insights for Language Models 🔬

summaries.summary: RAIL introduces a human-centric evaluation framework for large audio-language models, focusing on auditory cognition through five core capabilities. Evaluating 26 state-of-the-art models reveals significant performance disparities across these cognitive abilities, highlighting the need for more comprehensive assessment methods.
summaries.suggested_subject: New RAIL Benchmark Evaluates Auditory Intelligence in AI Models 🔬

summaries.summary: Subliminal learning in language model distillation transfers both benign and undesirable behaviors, which this study quantifies using two teacher models. Evaluation shows Llama-2 has a sharp transfer threshold while Qwen2.5 exhibits continuous transfer, with ratios reaching up to 0.61.
summaries.suggested_subject: Quantifying Subliminal Learning in Language Model Distillation 🔬

summaries.summary: Corpus poisoning attacks in Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems are less effective after reranking due to fragmented adversarial signals. The proposed Chunk-aware and Rerank-Consistent Poisoning framework achieves higher attack success rates and robustness across realistic retrieval pipelines.
summaries.suggested_subject: Enhanced Poisoning Attacks in RAG Systems 🔬

summaries.summary: OmniBioTwin introduces a System-of-Twinned-Systems framework for health digital twins, enabling modular and coordinated modeling across multiple biological scales. Demonstrated with GLP-1 signaling pathways in Alzheimer's, it integrates data and decision support for improved patient-specific insights.
summaries.suggested_subject: Modular Health Digital Twins Framework Boosts Patient Modeling 🔬

summaries.summary: Federated Continual Learning (FCL) combines privacy-preserving federated learning with continual learning to address the challenges of evolving data distributions across distributed clients. This comprehensive survey outlines FCL's unique characteristics, limitations of classical methods, and proposes a taxonomy while identifying key challenges for real-world applications.
summaries.suggested_subject: Federated Continual Learning Tackles Non-Stationary Data Challenges 🔍

summaries.summary: RoVE modifies Rotary Position Embeddings to make value pathways position-sensitive, enhancing attention mechanisms. Empirical results show improvements in few-shot learning and long-context retrieval for GPT-2 models, outperforming RoPE consistently.
summaries.suggested_subject: Enhanced Attention with Rotary Value Embeddings 🔬

summaries.summary: FreeBridge models cellular transition dynamics using a Schrödinger Bridge approach, enabling stochastic transport between control and treated populations based solely on endpoint data. It achieves competitive endpoint fidelity and reduces intermediate support violations, improving biological interpretability across multiple datasets.
summaries.suggested_subject: FreeBridge Enhances Single-Cell Transition Modeling with Geometry 🔬

summaries.summary: Embodied-R1.5 integrates embodied reasoning and task planning in a single architecture, utilizing a large-scale data system and a closed-loop framework for autonomous execution and self-correction. With 8B parameters, it outperforms leading models on 16 out of 24 benchmarks and excels in real-robot experiments across various manipulation tasks.
summaries.suggested_subject: Unified Model Achieves SOTA in Physical Intelligence Tasks 🔬

summaries.summary: FlowBank introduces a three-stage framework for optimizing agentic workflows by generating, compressing, and adaptively selecting reusable workflows. It outperforms existing methods, achieving a 4.26% improvement over the best automated baselines while maintaining cost-effectiveness.
summaries.suggested_subject: Optimized Workflows Boost LLM Efficiency by 4.26% 🔬

summaries.summary: A quantized stochastic primal-dual method, q-PDGD, optimizes distributed systems with finite-bit communication and stochastic gradients. It matches centralized stochastic rates in oracle complexity, achieving O(1/k) convergence with diminishing step sizes.
summaries.suggested_subject: Quantized Stochastic Optimization Achieves Linear Convergence 📊

summaries.summary: Introduced fragility as a metric to analyze language model pre-training beyond accuracy saturation. Fragility uncovers evolving representations and robustness gradients that accuracy alone cannot detect.
summaries.suggested_subject: New Metric Reveals Hidden Structure in LLM Training 🔍

summaries.summary: TileFuse is a mixed-precision kernel library designed for efficient quantized LLM inference on AMD XDNA2 NPUs, addressing the challenges of integrating low-bit formats with proprietary NPU software. It achieves up to 2.0x lower latency and over 64.6% energy savings in end-to-end LLM tasks compared to traditional methods.
summaries.suggested_subject: TileFuse Boosts LLM Inference Efficiency by 2× on AMD NPUs ⚡

summaries.summary: An auditable staged-promotion protocol for micro-pretraining minimizes experimental costs while maintaining effective configuration selection. The approach demonstrated a 66% reduction in GPU-hours compared to continuing all candidates, optimizing resource allocation without compromising performance.
summaries.suggested_subject: Staged Promotion Reduces Pretraining Costs by 66% ⚡

summaries.summary: Activation steering addresses state inertia in full-duplex spoken language models, enhancing their responsiveness during interruptions. This method improves response correctness from 28% to 45% and initial-word occurrence rate from 40% to 72% on the PersonaPlex benchmark.
summaries.suggested_subject: Boosting Full-Duplex Language Models' Interrupt Handling ⚡

summaries.summary: A semantic-timescale analysis pipeline quantifies how specificity and similarity in speech change over time, enabling comparisons between human and AI-generated language. Longer autocorrelation windows correlate with more generic vocabulary, while shorter windows indicate specific word usage, highlighting the importance of temporal organization in semantic content.
summaries.suggested_subject: New Semantic Timescale Analysis Reveals Speech Dynamics 🔬

summaries.summary: Instruction-based vector steering redistributes attention in large audio-language models to focus on acoustically relevant regions. This method achieves up to 68.72% overlap with ground-truth sound event intervals, significantly outperforming traditional prompting techniques.
summaries.suggested_subject: Instruction-Based Steering Enhances Audio Attention Focus 🔊

summaries.summary: Signed compression progress on a sealed audit ensures that agents are rewarded only for genuine learning improvements. Experiments show this method effectively prevents reward manipulation, maintaining audit integrity with a deviation scaling of n^{-0.527}.
summaries.suggested_subject: Intrinsic Reward Mechanism Resists Exploitation in AI Learning ⚡

summaries.summary: JailbreakOPT improves iterative prompt optimization for jailbreak attacks on large language models by organizing diverse prompts into a tool library and using contextual bandit strategies for better exploration. It achieves a higher attack success rate while reducing the number of attempts needed for success compared to previous methods.
summaries.suggested_subject: Enhanced Jailbreak Attacks with Tool-Assisted Optimization ⚡

summaries.summary: MPC-Patch-Bench introduces a benchmark for evaluating Large Language Model code repair specifically for Secure Multi-Party Computation software, addressing gaps in existing benchmarks. The strongest LLM resolves only 22.9% of tasks, with the MPC Verifier reducing verified resolutions to 17.1%, highlighting significant challenges in cryptographic safety and numerical fidelity.
summaries.suggested_subject: New Benchmark for Secure Code Repair in MPC 🔬

summaries.summary: A compute-aware evaluation framework quantifies the true cost of adversarial attacks on language models by measuring computational effort in floating-point operations (FLOPs). Findings show that attack effectiveness varies significantly with model size and alignment training, with costs differing by up to 5× across harm categories.
summaries.suggested_subject: Compute-Aware Evaluation Reveals Adversarial Attack Costs ⚡

summaries.summary: A relational bridge-database aligns bibliographic metadata with formal proof libraries, enhancing access to mathematical knowledge. A proposed formalization score quantifies coverage of publications in formal systems, facilitating large-scale analysis and integration of mathematical literature and machine-verifiable proofs.
summaries.suggested_subject: Bridging Math Literature and Formal Proofs with a New Database 🔗

summaries.summary: Test-time training (TTT) adapts model weights during inference to improve sampling from complex probability distributions. A quadratic lower bound on query complexity is established, showing optimality of existing methods, while a bounded class of distributions allows for improved sampling efficiency.
summaries.suggested_subject: Test-Time Training Boosts Sampling Efficiency ⚡

summaries.summary: APEX optimizes prompt engineering by dynamically selecting and stratifying data for large language models. It achieves an average performance improvement of 11.2% on Gemini 2.5 Flash with a fixed budget of 5,000 evaluation calls.
summaries.suggested_subject: Dynamic Data Selection Boosts Prompt Optimization by 11.2% 🔬

summaries.summary: LLM-based coding agents demonstrate comparable or greater methodological diversity than human analysts in social science research. Despite this, the agents remain susceptible to biases in interpretation, with significant shifts in verdicts based on prompts without altering underlying estimates.
summaries.suggested_subject: AI Agents Match Human Methodological Diversity in Social Science 🔬

summaries.summary: Long Short Term Memory (LSTM) neural networks detect structural breaks in property insurance loss reserving, outperforming traditional methods like Chain Ladder and Bornhuetter Ferguson. A targeted improvement of 15-20% in reserve accuracy for catastrophe-exposed years is anticipated, enhancing insurer solvency amidst climate-driven catastrophes.
summaries.suggested_subject: LSTM Improves Insurance Loss Reserving Accuracy by 20% 📊

summaries.summary: CRUMB optimizes prior-fitted network inference by clustering test queries and selecting a matched training subset, significantly reducing the computational burden. On the TabArena benchmark, CRUMB outperforms existing context selection methods, demonstrating improved efficiency and resilience to covariate drift.
summaries.suggested_subject: CRUMB Boosts PFN Inference Efficiency by Reducing Context Size ⚡

summaries.summary: Vision-language foundation models enable accurate and fair grading of handwritten exams. A benchmark shows a new model achieves 98.4% accuracy with a false-negative rate of just 0.58%.
summaries.suggested_subject: Automated Exam Grading Achieves 98.4% Accuracy 📊

summaries.summary: WebGraphMix selects pretraining data based on web graph centrality, optimizing the mix of central and peripheral documents without requiring labeled data. A 1:1 mixture of central and peripheral documents achieves an average performance of 41.4%, surpassing uniform sampling by 1.6%.
summaries.suggested_subject: Web Graph Centrality Boosts Language Model Performance by 3% 📊

summaries.summary: Evaluating deep learning models for detecting spoofing in facial recognition, MobileNetV2 outperforms others with 92% accuracy. This efficiency makes it suitable for real-world biometric security applications.
summaries.suggested_subject: MobileNetV2 Achieves 92% Accuracy in Biometric Spoofing Detection 🔒

summaries.summary: SirenFNO introduces a framework using sinusoidal representation networks to learn full-grid spectra for Fourier neural operators, eliminating frequency truncation. It achieves 4 to 15 times parameter reduction while maintaining performance across various PDE benchmarks.
summaries.suggested_subject: SirenFNO Boosts PDE Learning Efficiency by 15× 📊

summaries.summary: Language models role-playing historical figures express beliefs aligned with those personas, but this does not significantly alter their internal representations of truth. While role-play influences what models say, it does so with less internal belief change compared to models exhibiting Emergent Misalignment, which can shift false claims closer to truth.
summaries.suggested_subject: Role-Playing Alters Model Outputs, Not Internal Beliefs 🔍

summaries.summary: ISE introduces a three-stage synthesis method for training OS agents, generating structured user intents and authentic multi-turn interactions. Fine-tuning on the ISE dataset improves task performance from 19.3% to 37.7% pass rate, surpassing larger models like GPT-4o.
summaries.suggested_subject: New Dataset Boosts OS-Agent Performance by 95% 🚀

summaries.summary: A framework for classifying depressive states using EEG and fNIRS signals is developed, addressing the limitations of traditional diagnostic methods. This pilot study demonstrates the potential for objective, automated diagnostics in mental healthcare, paving the way for improved patient outcomes.
summaries.suggested_subject: Automated Depression Detection Using EEG and fNIRS ⚡

summaries.summary: A federated autoencoder system enables real-time ECG anomaly detection while ensuring privacy and efficiency on edge devices. It achieves an AUROC of 0.782 with 44% latency reduction on Raspberry Pi 4, maintaining strong privacy guarantees.
summaries.suggested_subject: Privacy-Preserving ECG Anomaly Detection on Edge Devices 🔒

summaries.summary: Pretrained self-supervised speech models are fine-tuned to recognize click consonants from Khoisan languages. Fine-tuned models achieve higher accuracy on click sounds compared to non-clicks, demonstrating effective generalization to rare phonemes.
summaries.suggested_subject: Self-Supervised Models Accurately Recognize Unseen Click Consonants 🔊

summaries.summary: Consistency Planner tackles real-time planning for autonomous driving using fast-sampling consistency models to generate diverse future trajectories. It outperforms existing methods in safety metrics, especially in complex dynamic scenarios.
summaries.suggested_subject: Real-time Planning Boosted by Fast-Sampling Models ⚡

summaries.summary: A hierarchical control design strategy ensures robust performance in networked systems using both model-based and data-driven approaches. The method achieves dissipativity guarantees while optimizing costs, demonstrated effectively in a DC microgrid application.
summaries.suggested_subject: Robust Control Design for Networked Systems ⚡

summaries.summary: Graph-native AI systems leverage large language models (LLMs) for structured reasoning and multi-hop inference. This integration enhances applications across various domains by improving retrieval, decision making, and factual consistency.
summaries.suggested_subject: Integrating LLMs with Graphs for Enhanced AI Systems 🔗

summaries.summary: A knowledge distillation framework integrates physics insights from literature into a lightweight neural network for predicting manufacturing process-property relationships. This approach achieves over 6000 Hz inference speed while maintaining high predictive accuracy and robustness in data-scarce scenarios.
summaries.suggested_subject: High-Accuracy Manufacturing Predictions via Physics-Distilled Neural Networks ⚡

summaries.summary: AVIS optimizes inference costs in Vision-Language Models by jointly adapting visual context and reasoning search. It enhances accuracy while reducing compute and latency across various benchmarks, outperforming traditional methods focused on a single axis.
summaries.suggested_subject: Adaptive Inference Scaling Boosts Vision-Language Model Efficiency ⚡

summaries.summary: A novel framework, Decomposition-based Multimodal Interaction Learning (DMIL), models sample-specific interactions to enhance multimodal learning. Extensive experiments show DMIL consistently outperforms existing methods across various tasks and architectures.
summaries.suggested_subject: Dynamic Multimodal Interaction Learning Boosts Performance 📊

summaries.summary: AI researchers must engage in arms control to mitigate risks from military AI applications. Collaborating with defense experts can help establish safety measures, drawing lessons from historical arms control efforts.
summaries.suggested_subject: AI Researchers Urged to Lead Military Arms Control ⚔️
